title: orchis × parvifolia subsp. timbali
intro: Orchis × parvifolia subsp. timbali is a plant species classified as a hybrid orchid, specifically a cross between the two parent species Orchis palustris and Orchis punctata. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, derived from the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, fever, and digestive problems, due to its health benefits such as expectorant and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
